assets.name: lecture_01_figure_02.png
assets.rationale: This is the most useful frame because it contains the most finished and legible form of the notation being introduced. Earlier candidates are mostly blank board or only the initial stroke of the symbol, while this frame clearly shows \(\lambda\), the equals sign, and the beginning of the written explanation, making it the best documentary evidence for the notation at this moment.

assets.name: lecture_01_figure_03.png
assets.rationale: This is the clearest unobstructed frame. It shows the full right-side board content relevant to the subtitle, with both equations legible and no lecturer, hand, or paper blocking the notation. The later candidate adds no new information, while the earlier ones are partially occluded.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: you can tell me the wavelength by solving this equation.

assets.name: lecture_01_figure_04.png
assets.rationale: This is the clearest and most complete frame. The final boxed equation is fully visible, the intermediate relations used to obtain it remain legible above, and the lecturer no longer blocks the right-hand mathematics. Earlier candidates are more occluded because he is still drawing the box or standing directly over the equations.

assets.name: lecture_01_figure_05.png
assets.rationale: This frame is the most useful because the key equation is fully visible and unobstructed while Susskind is explicitly indicating it. In the later candidates he steps in front of the board and blocks the notation he is adding, so they are less useful as documentary evidence for the notes.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: In this equation, it's rest mass and rest energy.
